principles: Maintain a calorie deficit (expend more energy than consumed).
Consume adequate protein (1.6g - 2.2g per kg of bodyweight) to preserve lean muscle tissue.
Perform resistance training at least 3-4 times a week to stimulate muscle retention.
Prioritize sleep (7-8 hours) to regulate hunger hormones (leptin and ghrelin).
Increase Non-Exercise Activity Thermogenesis (NEAT) by targetting daily step counts.
Choose whole, high-volume, single-ingredient foods to maximize satiety.
Maintain a slight caloric surplus (10% to 15% above maintenance).
Consume high protein (2.0g - 2.4g per kg of bodyweight) spaced evenly across 4-5 meals.
Apply progressive overload (gradually increasing weight, reps, or volume over time).
Target each major muscle group 2 times per week with structured hypertrophy splits.
Prioritize deep recovery, including 8+ hours of quality sleep and rest days.
Stay hydrated (3-4 liters of water daily) to optimize cell volume and performance.
Utilize Spaced Repetition (reviewing concepts at expanding intervals to build long-term memory).
Practice Active Recall (testing yourself instead of passively reading text or slides).
Divide complex programming/algorithmic structures into smaller sub-problems.
Code solutions from scratch without copying, and analyze time/space complexity (Big O).
Maintain consistent daily practice (even 1-2 solved problems) to build pattern recognition.
Conduct post-mortems on failed solutions to understand logic and edge-case errors.
Dedicate uninterrupted time (90-minute blocks) to building deep, high-value technical skills.
Document your progress publicly (write blogs, build open-source projects, compile portfolio).
Engage in strategic networking (reach out to industry peers, ask for feedback, participate in meetups).
Optimize resume and online profiles (LinkedIn, GitHub) with clear projects and metrics.
Practice mock interviewing (both system design and behavioral rounds) weekly.
Build soft skills, particularly technical communication, presentation, and team collaboration.
Time-box your schedule (plan exactly when and where you will perform tasks).
Eliminate environmental distractions (phone notifications, social media blocking) during work blocks.
Focus on a single task at a time (avoid multitasking, which incurs switching costs).
Apply the 80/20 rule (identify and execute the 20% of actions that yield 80% of results).
Perform weekly reviews to evaluate completed tasks and align the upcoming week's priority list.
Adopt the 'Two-Minute Rule' for small tasks: if it takes less than 2 minutes, do it immediately.
Maintain a consistent sleep-wake schedule, even on weekends, to align circadian rhythms.
Seek bright sunlight exposure within 30 minutes of waking to optimize morning cortisol.
Avoid blue light (screens, phones) and bright overhead lights for 1-2 hours before bed.
Keep the sleeping environment cool (around 18C / 65F), dark, and quiet.
Limit caffeine intake after 12:00 PM (noon) to ensure complete clearance before sleep.
Implement a relaxing evening wind-down routine (reading, stretching, journaling) to lower heart rate.
Incorporate daily mindfulness, breathing exercises, or meditation to activate the parasympathetic system.
Keep a gratitude journal to shift focus from stressors to positive elements.
Ensure regular physical activity (walking, cycling) to stimulate dopamine and serotonin.
Establish clear boundaries between work/study and leisure to prevent burnout.
Seek daily social connection (speaking with family, friends, or team members).
Allocate dedicated downtime for hobbies and creative activities that do not require achievement pressure.
Pay yourself first: automatically route a percentage of income to savings/investment on payday.
Track all daily expenses to identify and trim non-essential spending leaks.
Build an emergency fund covering 3-6 months of essential living expenses.
Invest consistently in low-cost index funds to harness compound interest.
Avoid high-interest consumer debt (credit cards, personal loans) entirely.
Set clear, mid-to-long term financial goals (saving for house, tuition, or retirement) and track milestones.
Drink adequate water (30-35ml per kg of bodyweight) throughout the day.
Eat a diverse range of whole foods, focusing on fiber, micronutrients, and lean proteins.
Engage in at least 150 minutes of moderate cardiovascular exercise per week.
Move regularly: take short active breaks (stretching, walking) for every hour of sitting.
Practice dental and physical hygiene consistency daily.
Engage in preventive healthcare routines (stretching, posture corrections, annual checkups).
